description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a double-wing configuration, featuring a light blue fuselage and dark blue wings adorned with orange and blue roundels. The nose is painted orange and houses a visible wooden propeller, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield for the pilot. The tail section is streamlined, with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both in dark blue with white accents. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, all fixed and exposed. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military or stunt aircraft, with a compact and robust build suitable for short takeoffs and landings. The surface appears metallic with painted details, and the structure is likely composed of lightweight metal and fabric.
description: A vintage biplane with blue and orange roundels, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear. The fuselage is light blue, and the wings are dark blue with distinctive markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the orange nose, wooden propeller, open cockpit, and the double-wing structure with roundels on the upper wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wing stagger, roundel markings, and the streamlined tail with visible landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, single vertical stabilizer, and the open cockpit behind the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and the detailed wing and tail structure with roundel markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, staggered wings, no visible winglets, and slight dihedral on the lower wing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit with a small windshield, located just behind the upper wing.
